query: Ilya Sutskever Safe Superintelligence research infrastructure cloud provider partnership

mako.raw:
{"query":"Ilya Sutskever Safe Superintelligence research infrastructure cloud provider partnership","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1146,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/safe-superintelligence-partnership-google-cloud-ssi-ai-6wwbf","title":"Safe Superintelligence Partnership with Google Cloud","content":"Safe Superintelligence is partnering with Google Cloud to use TPUs to accelerate its research and development efforts toward building a safe, superintelligent AI.
Cloud providers are chasing a handful of unicorn AI startups that spend hundreds of millions of dollars on computing power every year to train AI foundation models.
(Sutskever once worked at Google.) In October, Google Cloud said it would be the primary computing provider for World Labs, founded by ex-Google Cloud AI chief scientist Fei-Fei Li.
The company has $1 billion in backing from Andreessen Horowitz, Sequoia Capital, DST Global, SV Angel, and others.
Before co-founding OpenAI, Sutskever spent several years at Google Brain researching neural networks."},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ilya-sutskever-taps-google-cloud-185513108.html","title":"Ilya Sutskever taps Google Cloud to power his AI startup's research","content":"It's unclear if SSI has struck partnerships with other cloud or computing providers.
SSI came out of stealth in June 2024, months after Sutskever departed from his role as OpenAI's chief scientist.
After leading OpenAI's AI safety work for years, Sutskever played a key role in the ousting of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman in November 2023."},{"url":"https://ssi.inc/","title":"Safe Superintelligence Inc.","content":"We are an American company with offices in Palo Alto and Tel Aviv, where we have deep roots and the ability to recruit top technical talent."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Safe_Superintelligence_Inc.","title":"Safe Superintelligence Inc. - Wikipedia","content":"On May 15, 2024, OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever left OpenAI after a board dispute where he voted to fire Sam Altman amid concerns about communication and trust.
On June 19, 2024, Sutskever posted on X that he was starting SSI Inc, with the goal to safely develop superintelligent AI, alongside Daniel Levy, and Daniel Gross.
In March 2025, SSI reached a $30 billion valuation in a funding round led by Greenoaks Capital.
In April 2025, Google Cloud announced a partnership to provide TPUs for SSI's research.
In July 2025, co-founder Gross left the company to join Meta Superintelligence Labs, and Sutskever became the CEO of SSI.
"OpenAI co-founder unveils new company focused on "safe superintelligence"".
"Ilya Sutskever taps Google Cloud to power his AI startup's research"."},{"url":"https://www.calcalistech.com/ctechnews/article/sjik6rzdwl","title":"Ilya Sutskever’s Safe Superintelligence engineer walks away to fix what AI breaks | Ctech","content":"Shahar Papini left the $32 billion research lab to co-found Attestable, which has raised $18.5 million to verify AI models, prompts and outputs.
Papini, who spent roughly a year as a senior software engineer at SSI following its creation in 2024, has joined Attestable as co-founder and chief technology officer.
The Tel Aviv-based company, still operating in stealth, has raised $18.5 million in Seed funding, including backing from TLV Partners, according to PitchBook.
SSI, which maintains a significant team in Tel Aviv, has raised more than $3 billion to pursue what Sutskever describes as a fundamentally new path to artificial general intelligence, one that moves beyond the industry’s fixation on ever-larger models and brute-force scaling."}]}

Superintelligence Inc.","content":"**Safe Superintelligence Inc.** (**SSI Inc.**) is an Israeli-American artificial intelligence company founded by Ilya Sutskever, the former chief scientist of OpenAI; Daniel Gross \"Daniel Gross (entrepreneur)\"), former head of Apple’s AI efforts; and Daniel Levy, an investor and former AI researcher at OpenAI. The company's mission is to focus on safely developing a superintelligence, a computer-based agent capable of surpassing human intelligence. On May 15, 2024, OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever left OpenAI after a board dispute where he voted to fire Sam Altman amid concerns about communication and trust. On June 19, 2024, Sutskever posted on X that he was starting SSI Inc, with the goal to safely develop superintelligent AI, alongside Daniel Levy, and Daniel Gross. In July 2025, co-founder Gross left the company to join Meta Superintelligence Labs, and Sutskever became the CEO of SSI. 1. ↑ \"Exclusive: OpenAI co-founder Sutskever's new safety-focused AI startup SSI raises $1 billion\". \"OpenAI's former chief scientist is starting a new AI company\".","score":0.57035583,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.calcalistech.com/ctechnews/article/sy8f11qr0jx","title":"Safe Superintelligence and Eon join Google Cloud to advance AI safety ...","content":"Safe Superintelligence Inc.
